heading: Channybearup vs Charley Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Channybearup, Western Australia and Charley Creek, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Charley Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Channybearup (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Channybearup or Charley Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Channybearup has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Charley Creek scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Channybearup scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Charley Creek scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Channybearup or Charley Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Charley Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
